1→# Jiranimo Development Rules
     2→
     3→## TDD Workflow
     4→
     5→**Build → Test → Verify → Next.** This is non-negotiable.
     6→
     7→1. When implementing a module, write its tests immediately after (in the same session)
     8→2. Every `.ts` source file in `server/src/` gets a co-located `.test.ts` sibling (except `types.ts` files)
     9→3. Every `.js` source file in `extension/lib/` and `extension/content/` gets a `.test.js` sibling
    10→4. Run `cd server && npm test` after completing each module — all tests must pass before moving on
    11→5. Never move to the next implementation phase until the current phase's tests are green
    12→6. Use the `test` agent proactively after writing code
    13→7. Use the `verify` agent before committing
    14→
    15→## Project Structure
    16→
    17→- `server/` — Node.js/TypeScript backend (Express, Claude Code executor, git/PR workflow)
    18→- `extension/` — Chrome extension (Manifest V3, vanilla JS)
    19→- Tests are co-located next to source files, not in a separate test directory (except E2E and integration tests)
    20→
    21→## Test Tiers
    22→
    23→- `npm test` — unit tests (fast, every commit)
    24→- `npm run test:integration` — integration tests with fake Claude subprocess + real git on temp repos
    25→- `npm run test:e2e` — E2E tests against real Jira site (manual/nightly)
    26→
    27→## Bug Fix Protocol
    28→
    29→Every bug fix MUST include:
    30→1. A new or updated test that reproduces the bug (fails before the fix)
    31→2. The fix itself
    32→3. Run the test to verify it passes
    33→4. Post the screenshot path that proves the fix works
    34→
    35→Never fix a bug without adding a test. The test should specifically cover the scenario that caused the bug.
    36→
    37→## Chrome Extension Reload
    38→
    39→After modifying ANY file in `extension/`, ALWAYS ask the user to reload the extension before testing:
    40→> "Reload the extension in chrome://extensions (click the reload icon on Jiranimo) before testing."
    41→
    42→Chrome does not auto-reload unpacked extensions. Without a reload, changes won't take effect.
    43→
    44→## Jira API Access Rules
    45→
    46→All Jira API calls in the extension MUST go through the **content script** using `fetch` with `credentials: 'include'` (session cookies). Never use the background service worker for Jira API calls — it doesn't have access to session cookies (especially in incognito).
    47→
    48→The **only** exception: E2E test setup/cleanup code (`test/e2e/jira-helpers.ts`) uses API token auth to create/delete test data. This is test infrastructure only, not production code.
    49→
    50→## Conventions
    51→
    52→- Server uses TypeScript with strict mode
    53→- Extension uses vanilla JS (no build step, Manifest V3)
    54→- State stored in JSON file at `~/.jiranimo/state.json`, not a database
    55→- All Jira API communication happens in the Chrome extension (session cookies), never on the server
    56→- Server config in `jiranimo.config.json`, extension config in `chrome.storage.local`
    57→

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile the full overview: ## Comprehensive Jiranimo Repository Overview ### 1. **Application Type** Jiranimo is a **hybrid Chrome extension + Node.js server application** that integrates Claude AI code generation with Jira sprint boards. It automatically implements Jira tasks using Claude Code. **Architecture:** - **Frontend:** Chrome Extension (Manifest V3) running on Jira sprint board pages - **Backend:** Node.js/TypeScript Express server with WebSocket support - **Dashboard:** Web UI at `localhost:3456` to monitor task execution ### 2. **Project Structure** ``` /Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/ ├── extension/ # Chrome Extension (Manifest V3) │ ├── background.js # Service worker - handles extension settings, dev auto-reload │ ├── content/ # Content scripts (run on Jira board pages) │ │ ├── content.js # Main logic - scans cards, injects badges, listens for WS messages │ │ ├── content.css # Overlay/modal styles, badge animations │ │ └── board-config.js # Configuration modal for first-time board setup │ ├── lib/ # Libraries │ │ ├── adf-to-markdown.js # Jira ADF → Markdown converter │ │ └── jira-api.js # Jira REST API wrapper │ ├── options/ # Extension settings page │ │ ├── options.html # HTML UI │ │ └── options.js # Settings management │ ├── manifest.json # Extension manifest │ └── icons/ # Extension icons │ ├── server/ # Node.js/TypeScript backend │ ├── src/ │ │ ├── index.ts # Entry point, server initialization │ │ ├── web/ # Express app & routes │ │ │ ├── server.ts # App creation (CORS, middleware, MCP, API routes, static files) │ │ │ ├── api-routes.ts # REST API endpoints (/api/tasks, etc.) │ │ │ ├── ws-handler.ts # WebSocket message handler with offline queue │ │ │ └── public/ # Dashboard static files │ │ │ ├── index.html # Dashboard UI │ │ │ ├── style.css # Dark theme dashboard styles │ │ │ └── app.js # Client-side dashboard logic │ │ ├── pipeline/ # Task execution pipeline (state machine) │ │ ├── claude/ # Claude API integration │ │ ├── state/ # State store (JSON file-based) │ │ ├── config/ # Configuration loader │ │ └── mcp/ # MCP server for Claude Code callback │ └── package.json │ ├── package.json # Monorepo (workspaces: server, extension) └── CLAUDE.md # Development rules & conventions ``` ### 3. **Main Entry Points** **Chrome Extension (Content Script - runs on Jira board):** - **File:** `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/content.js` - **Purpose:** Detects Jira cards, injects "Implement" badges, handles badge clicks, polls server via WebSocket - **Key functions:** - `init()` - Loads board config, starts scanning - `scanCards()` - Finds cards with trigger label, injects badges - `handleBadgeClick()` - Submits task to server - `connectWebSocket()` - Real-time task status updates **Express Server:** - **File:** `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/server/src/index.ts` - **Listens on:** `http://localhost:3456` - **Provides:** - REST API at `/api/tasks` - WebSocket at `/ws` - Dashboard at `/` - MCP endpoint at `/mcp` (Claude Code integration) ### 4. **Board Screen UI Components** The board/main view exists in two places: **On Jira Board (Extension Content Script):** - **Location:** `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/content.js` + `/extension/content/content.css` - **Components:** - **Badge icon** (sparkles SVG): Injected next to each task title with animated states - `idle` (blue) - Click to implement - `queued` (orange pulse) - Waiting for execution - `in-progress` (blue sparkle animation) - Running - `completed` (green) - Task complete - `failed` (red) - Failed, click to retry - **Config modal** (first-time setup): - Shows overlay with Jira column selectors - Styled: `jiranimo-config-overlay` + `jiranimo-config-modal` **Dashboard (Web Browser):** - **Location:** `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/server/src/web/public/` - **HTML:** `index.html` (simple grid layout with 4 sections: Queued, In Progress, Completed, Failed) - **CSS:** `style.css` (dark theme, task cards with status indicators) - **JavaScript:** `app.js` (no keyboard handlers present) ### 5. **HTML/CSS/JS Files Summary** | File Path | Type | Purpose | |-----------|------|---------| | `extension/content/content.js` | JS | Main content script (badge injection, task submission, WS listener) | | `extension/content/content.css` | CSS | Badge animations, overlay/modal styling | | `extension/content/board-config.js` | JS | Board configuration modal | | `extension/background.js` | JS | Service worker (dev auto-reload, server fetch proxy) | | `extension/options/options.html` | HTML | Extension settings page | | `extension/options/options.js` | JS | Settings management | | `server/src/web/public/index.html` | HTML | Dashboard layout (4 task sections) | | `server/src/web/public/style.css` | CSS | Dashboard dark theme | | `server/src/web/public/app.js` | JS | Dashboard client (fetch tasks, WS messages, render) | ### 6. **Existing Keyboard Shortcut Handling** **Current Status:** NO keyboard shortcuts currently implemented in the codebase. Searched patterns: - `keydown`, `keypress`, `addEventListener.*key` - No matches found - The board extension and dashboard have no keyboard event listeners **Opportunity:** Keyboard shortcuts (like `cmd+e` for about page) would need to be added fresh. ### 7. **Existing Overlay/Modal Components** **Modal Pattern 1: Board Configuration Modal** (Best example to follow) - **File:** `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/board-config.js` (lines 28-92) - **Structure:** ```javascript // Create overlay (dark backdrop with fixed positioning) const overlay = document.createElement('div'); overlay.className = 'jiranimo-config-overlay'; // Create modal (centered white box inside overlay) const modal = document.createElement('div'); modal.className = 'jiranimo-config-modal'; modal.innerHTML = `

Title

`; overlay.appendChild(modal); document.body.appendChild(overlay); // Close on overlay background click overlay.addEventListener('click', (e) => { if (e.target === overlay) overlay.remove(); }); ``` - **CSS Styling** (`extension/content/content.css`, lines 64-121): - `.jiranimo-config-overlay`: Fixed positioning, semi-transparent dark backdrop (rgba 0.54 opacity), z-index 10000 - `.jiranimo-config-modal`: White background, centered, 420px width (max 90vw), box-shadow, border-radius 8px - Uses Atlassian-inspired color palette (blue #0052cc, grays) **Modal Pattern 2: Tooltip/Title attributes** - Badge icons use `title` attribute for status messages - Not a full modal, but standard browser UI ### 8. **Message Flow Architecture** **Badge Click → Task Submission:** ``` Jira Board (Extension) ↓ User clicks "Implement" badge ↓ content.js: handleBadgeClick() ↓ Fetch full issue details from Jira REST API ↓ POST /api/tasks to server (via serverFetch → background worker proxy) ↓ Server: Creates task, starts pipeline ↓ WebSocket: Server broadcasts 'task-created' message ↓ Extension receives WS message, updates badge state ↓ Board transitions Jira card (if configured) ``` **Real-time Updates:** - Server sends updates via WebSocket to extension - Extension updates badge visual state - Dashboard also listens to same WebSocket for live task grid ### 9. **Relevant Files for Implementing About Page Overlay** To implement a `cmd+e` keyboard-triggered about page overlay on the board, you would need: 1. **Keyboard Handler** (add to `extension/content/content.js`): - **Dashboard:** Web UI at `localhost:3456` to monitor task execution ### 2. **Project Structure** ``` /Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/ ├── extension/ # Chrome Extension (Manifest V3) │ ├── background.js # Service worker - handles extension settings, dev auto-reload │ ├── content/ # Content scripts (run on Jira board pages) │ │ ├── content.js # Main logic - scans cards, injects badges, listens for WS messages │ │ ├── content.css # Overlay/modal styles, badge animations │ │ └── board-config.js # Configuration modal for first-time board setup │ ├── lib/ # Libraries │ │ ├── adf-to-markdown.js # Jira ADF → Markdown converter │ │ └── jira-api.js # Jira REST API wrapper │ ├── options/ # Extension settings page │ │ ├── options.html # HTML UI │ │ └── options.js # Settings management │ ├── manifest.json # Extension manifest │ └── icons/ # Extension icons │ ├── server/ # Node.js/TypeScript backend │ ├── src/ │ │ ├── index.ts # Entry point, server initialization │ │ ├── web/ # Express app & routes │ │ │ ├── server.ts # App creation (CORS, middleware, MCP, API routes, static files) │ │ │ ├── api-routes.ts # REST API endpoints (/api/tasks, etc.) │ │ │ ├── ws-handler.ts # WebSocket message handler with offline queue │ │ │ └── public/ # Dashboard static files │ │ │ ├── index.html # Dashboard UI │ │ │ ├── style.css # Dark theme dashboard styles │ │ │ └── app.js # Client-side dashboard logic │ │ ├── pipeline/ # Task execution pipeline (state machine) │ │ ├── claude/ # Claude API integration │ │ ├── state/ # State store (JSON file-based) │ │ ├── config/ # Configuration loader │ │ └── mcp/ # MCP server for Claude Code callback │ └── package.json │ ├── package.json # Monorepo (workspaces: server, extension) └── CLAUDE.md # Development rules & conventions ``` ### 3. **Main Entry Points** **Chrome Extension (Content Script - runs on Jira board):** - **File:** `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/content.js` - **Purpose:** Detects Jira cards, injects "Implement" badges, handles badge clicks, polls server via WebSocket - **Key functions:** - `init()` - Loads board config, starts scanning - `scanCards()` - Finds cards with trigger label, injects badges - `handleBadgeClick()` - Submits task to server - `connectWebSocket()` - Real-time task status updates **Express Server:** - **File:** `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/server/src/index.ts` - **Listens on:** `http://localhost:3456` - **Provides:** - REST API at `/api/tasks` - WebSocket at `/ws` - Dashboard at `/` - MCP endpoint at `/mcp` (Claude Code integration) ### 4. **Board Screen UI Components** The board/main view exists in two places: **On Jira Board (Extension Content Script):** - **Location:** `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/content.js` + `/extension/content/content.css` - **Components:** - **Badge icon** (sparkles SVG): Injected next to each task title with animated states - `idle` (blue) - Click to implement - `queued` (orange pulse) - Waiting for execution - `in-progress` (blue sparkle animation) - Running - `completed` (green) - Task complete - `failed` (red) - Failed, click to retry - **Config modal** (first-time setup): - Shows overlay with Jira column selectors - Styled: `jiranimo-config-overlay` + `jiranimo-config-modal` **Dashboard (Web Browser):** - **Location:** `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/server/src/web/public/` - **HTML:** `index.html` (simple grid layout with 4 sections: Queued, In Progress, Completed, Failed) - **CSS:** `style.css` (dark theme, task cards with status indicators) -
